prompt: The string concatenation inside a loop is slow. What is the fix?
choices: String concatenation is not allowed inside for loops
Each result += creates a new String object; for large n this is O(n^2). Use StringBuilder.append() instead for O(n) performance
The method should return char[] instead of String
String.format() should be used instead of concatenation
explanation: Strings are immutable; += inside a loop creates a new String each iteration, copying all previous content. For n iterations this is O(n^2) work. Fix: StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); for (...) sb.append(i).append(','); return sb.toString();
